response.justification: The candidate lacks the required 5-10 years of professional C++ development experience on Windows, with only limited C/C++ exposure in academic and embedded contexts. While the resume shows strong general software engineering skills and domain versatility, there is no clear evidence of fixed-income, capital markets, or investment banking domain expertise. The professional focus has been primarily on web, mobile, and embedded systems using languages like TypeScript, Node.js, D, and Java, which do not align closely with the role's technical stack.
strengths: Strong general software engineering background with diverse language proficiency (JavaScript, TypeScript, D, Java, C/C++)
Proven ability to work in agile environments with emphasis on testing (TDD, CI/CD)
Experience in financial services (RIDE Capital) though not directly in fixed-income systems
concerns: Insufficient professional C++ development experience on Windows platform (mandatory requirement)
No demonstrated fixed-income or capital markets domain expertise (mandatory requirement)
Primary expertise in non-C++ languages (TypeScript, Node.js, D) with limited Windows C++ development
